hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (492) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,471), and overheal (62%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (60.4%) by 1.6 points - active time (78.9% vs. 96.4% average, a real 17.5-point gap) stands out as the bigger issue here - landing at the 27th percentile, the lowest of the raid night, with 15 real deaths logged, by far the most of any kill this raid.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 46 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, sizeable gap, and the first of a consistent pattern across most of this raid night.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample. All 15 real deaths land nearest to the same single Earth Shield cast (targeted at Captinhelp, not any of them) purely because it's the only Earth Shield logged this kill - not a real save for any of the 15, just the nearest thing on record.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (77.1% vs. 77.5% average) tracked almost exactly on the Top 100 norm, while top-target concentration (33.4% vs. 47.7% average, -14.3) ran below typical, across 11 distinct targets.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,123) ran below the Top 100 average (1,759, a 64% ratio - the strongest HPS ratio of the raid night), with overheal (27.3%) running a real 14.3 points better than the sample's single worst parse (41.6%) - an outstanding 91st-percentile kill, the best of the raid night by a wide margin and ranking 1st of the tracked healers present by both real comparison metrics, despite 12 real deaths logged (the second-most of the raid night) - a real, notable case where raid-wide survivability and this healer's own output diverge.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 43.6 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, sizeable gap, consistent with the rest of the raid night.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample. The 12 real deaths cluster around three separate cooldown casts (Mana Tide Totem, Earth Shield, Mana Potion), none targeted at the specific players who died and none close enough to call a real save.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (43.7% vs. 52.1% average) and top-target concentration (15.7% vs. 16.8% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (802) ran below the Top 100 average (1,971), landing at a middling 50th percentile, with 10 real deaths logged (the third-most of the raid night), including Vajomee's own - Balancemaker died within 1 millisecond of Vajomee, suggesting a shared burst-damage moment rather than a healing failure specific to either.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 83.2 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the largest composition gap of the entire raid night.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched or exceeded the Top 100 sample - 5 real Earth Shields, the heaviest count of the raid night. Vajomee's own death and Balancemaker's both land nearest an Ancestral Swiftness cast from 9.3 seconds earlier - a self-buff cast before, not after, so not a save. The remaining 8 deaths show similar coincidental proximity to casts aimed at other players.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (41.8% vs. 51.2% average) and top-target concentration (8.6% vs. 14% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 23 distinct targets.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,009) ran below the Top 100 average (2,159), and overheal (34.8%) ran 9.7 points better than the sample's single worst parse (44.5%) - a middling 53rd-percentile kill, ranking 1st of the tracked healers present by item-level rank, with only 1 real death logged.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 49.7 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, sizeable gap.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Both Ancestral Swiftness (87%-used norm) and Mana Potion (57%-used norm) went completely uncast this kill - the only boss this raid night with two real cooldown gaps at once. The one real death (Megatauro) doesn't land close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (57.9% vs. 74% average, -13.3) ran a real gap below the Top 100 norm, while top-target concentration (26% vs. 34.5% average) tracked modestly below typical, across 23 distinct targets.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (822) ran below the Top 100 average (1,532), and overheal (49.8%) sits close to the sample's single worst parse (53.8%, a 4-point gap) - a solid 57th-percentile kill, ranking 1st of the tracked healers present by item-level rank, with 3 real deaths logged.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 76.5 points ahead of the Top 100 average - one of the largest composition gaps of the raid night.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample. None of the 3 real deaths (Porgrok, Ghostpepperx, Zuggnuggs) land close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (55.8% vs. 51.9% average) and top-target concentration (22.2% vs. 22.1% average) both landed almost exactly on the Top 100 norm, across 24 distinct targets - the widest spread of the raid night.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (579) ran below the Top 100 average (1,085), and overheal (45%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (41.2%) by 3.8 points - active time (79.2% vs. 94.2% average, a real 15-point gap) was also a real issue - on the raid's longest kill this night (498 seconds), landing at the 45th percentile, one of the raid's three lowest kills, with 6 real deaths logged.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 57.4 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, sizeable gap.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Earth Shield was cast 6 times (Captinhelp x2, Nameitis x3, Braila) - the heaviest use of the raid night, in line with the sample. Dark Rune went uncast against a real 50%-used norm here - the highest baseline Dark Rune usage of any boss this raid night, and a real gap. Ghostpepperx's death landed just 486 milliseconds before an Earth Shield cast aimed at a different player - coincidental, not a save.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (40.2% vs. 46.8% average) and top-target concentration (10.6% vs. 17.5% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 26 distinct targets - the widest spread of the raid night.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (521) ran below the Top 100 average (1,121), and overheal (52.8%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (50.6%) by 2.2 points - a strong 62nd-percentile kill, tied for the second-best of the raid night, with 4 real deaths logged.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran a comparatively modest 39.3 points ahead of the Top 100 average - the second-smallest composition gap of the raid night.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample. None of the 4 real deaths land close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (55.2% vs. 54.5% average) and top-target concentration (23.4% vs. 23.6% average) both landed almost exactly on the Top 100 norm, across 25 distinct targets.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,614) ran below the Top 100 average (2,650), and overheal (31.6%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (25%) by 6.6 points - a strong 62nd-percentile kill, tied for the second-best of the raid night, with 2 real deaths logged.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: This boss shows a real, boss-specific quirk not seen on any other kill this raid night: Chain Heal splits across two distinct real guids here. Vajomee's healing runs 44% under guid 10623 (the same guid used everywhere else this raid) plus another 37.7% under a second real guid, 25423 - a combined 81.7% Chain Heal share. The Top 100 sample on this specific boss runs the opposite ratio (83.9% under guid 25423, only 1.2% under guid 10623, a further 2.3% under a third real guid, 25422, that Vajomee didn't use at all) - a combined ~87.4%. So while the raw 'guid 25423' comparison shows a 46.2-point gap, Vajomee's total real Chain Heal reliance on this boss (81.7%) is actually close to the benchmark's total (87.4%) once both guids are counted - this is a guid-composition difference, not an undercast of Chain Heal itself.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against an 89%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap. Neither of the 2 real deaths (Silkyrebel, Leechers) lands close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (44.3% vs. 42.7% average) and top-target concentration (12.2% vs. 11% average) both landed almost exactly on the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (705) ran below the Top 100 average (1,654), and overheal (61.6%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (56.7%) by 4.9 points - the largest overheal-over-worst gap of the raid night - yet this landed at a solid 60th percentile on the raid's shortest kill (147 seconds), ranking 1st of the tracked healers present by raw healing, with zero real deaths logged.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Chain Heal's share ran 31.4 points ahead of the Top 100 average - a real, moderate gap.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against a 72%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap this kill. No deaths to answer.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (48.2% vs. 51.3% average) and top-target concentration (14.7% vs. 15.3% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 22 distinct targets, on a clean kill.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: fully consistent across all 9 kills - no gear or enchant variance of any kind this raid night
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: Two real gear gaps stand out: the Feet slot (item 28752) and the Back slot (item 28765) both carry no permanent enchant across every kill this raid night - the same two persistent gaps seen on Vajomee's other recent raid night, two cheap, static enchants that would apply immediately and don't require any fight-specific loadout choice.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: 9 of 10 real bosses attempted were killed this raid night - Kael'thas Sunstrider was attempted 4 times and never killed, the raid's only non-kill. This was a genuinely mixed but notably better raid night than Vajomee's other recent report: raw HPS still trailed the Top 100 average on every kill (33%-64% of the benchmark average), and Vajomee ranked 1st of the tracked healers present by item-level rank on 3 of 9 kills (56% average rank percentile) and 1st by raw healing on 2 of 9. Percentiles ranged widely, from 27th (Hydross, the lowest) to a genuinely outstanding 91st (The Lurker Below, the best of the raid night) - Hydross, Lady Vashj (45th), and Leotheras (50th) were the raid's three lowest kills. Chain Heal's composition share ran ahead of the Top 100 average on 8 of 9 kills (Void Reaver was the exception - a real, boss-specific two-guid Chain Heal split there, not an actual undercast, see that boss's own page). Overheal matched or exceeded the Top 100 sample's own single worst parse on 5 of 9 kills. Deaths were heaviest on Hydross (15, the most of the raid night) and The Lurker Below (12) - notably, Lurker's high death count came alongside Vajomee's own best individual performance of the night, a real disconnect between raid-wide survivability and this healer's own output. Vajomee's own single death this raid landed on Leotheras, within 1 millisecond of another player's death, suggesting a shared burst event rather than an isolated healing failure. Solarian was the only clean kill.

GearCheckItems.Description: Elixir of Healing Power (Battle) and Elixir of Draenic Wisdom (Guardian) both active on all 9 kills, plus Well Fed food buff
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A real, complete consumable setup across the whole raid night - the Battle + Guardian Elixir pairing is a fully valid alternative to a Flask under the real TBC rule.
